techniques.evidence_quote: the attacker first accessed Timehop's cloud environment in December — using compromised admin credentials
the attacker was able to compromise Timehop's cloud computing environment by targeting an account that had not been protected by multifactor authentication
apparently conducting reconnaissance for a few days that month, and again for another day in March and one in June
the personal data (names and emails) of 21 million users...Around a fifth of the affected users — or 4.7M — have also had a phone number that was attached to their account breached
the keys that allow it to read and show users their social media content were compromised
it is a high likelihood that they will soon appear [on the Dark Web]
using compromised admin credentials...targeting an account that had not been protected by multifactor authentication

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained and used compromised admin credentials (lacking MFA) to gain initial access to Timehop's cloud environment in December 2017.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker first accessed Timehop's cloud environment in December — using compromised admin credentials

attack_chain.description: Attacker conducted reconnaissance across Timehop's cloud environment over multiple sessions: a few days in December 2017, one day in March 2018, and one day in June 2018, mapping out the infrastructure before the main attack.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: apparently conducting reconnaissance for a few days that month, and again for another day in March and one in June

attack_chain.description: On July 4, 2018, the attacker accessed Timehop's cloud-hosted user database and exfiltrated the personal data (names, emails, phone numbers) of 21 million users.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: compromised the personal data (names and emails) of 21 million users...Around a fifth of the affected users — or 4.7M — have also had a phone number...breached in the attack

attack_chain.description: During the same July 4 attack window, the attacker stole OAuth tokens (social media API keys) that granted access to users' social media profiles on platforms like Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the keys that allow it to read and show users their social media content were compromised

initial_access_vector: Compromised admin credentials used to authenticate directly to the cloud environment; the targeted account lacked multi-factor authentication enforcement.
persistence_mechanism: Repeated low-profile access sessions over multiple months (December 2017, March 2018, June 2018) using the same compromised admin credentials before executing the main attack on July 4, 2018.
privilege_escalation: Not explicitly described; attacker already held admin-level credentials from initial compromise, granting broad cloud environment access without needing further escalation.
